“

Carol Holland has been a phenomenal resource for me. I have a very complex medical picture following a spinal cord injury 20 years ago. She is not only very knowledgeable and focused about continuing to expand her herbal directions — but she is also more hardworking and dedicated to her patients than anyone I have ever run across. She has helped me manage difficult dental pain, immune support and general inflammation problems, as well as chronic bowel and bladder issues caused by my SCI. I am grateful that she does not shy away from such challenges and continues to think outside the box. I would not hesitate to consult her.

— CLIENT TESTIMONIAL (ANONYMOUS)

ABOUT

A Glimpse of the Herbalist

I'm Carol Holland — a Clinical Herbalist based in Bellingham, Washington, with over a decade of experience with botanicals, learning from many of the most respected and experienced herbalists in the US, with past clinical experience as an RN & Research Nurse, and a degree in the Biological sciences.

As chronic illnesses became more prevalent and I faced personal and family health challenges, I was led to explore alternative options to wellness. This journey revealed to me the remarkable power of botanicals which, when coupled with the body's innate healing potential, offered enhanced opportunities for a more secure route toward lasting health, and has manifested in my dedication to bring botanical perspectives to those who are searching for options to wellness.

My herbal training has primarily been in the Western tradition, but I also draw from other overlapping herbal disciplines such as TCM and Ayurveda, as well as relying on nutraceutical supplements for support.

My approach is simple: listen intently, identify root causes, and establish a plan that fits your lifestyle.

“I imagine if we acknowledged that everything we consume is the gift of Mother Earth, we would take better care of what we are given.”
Robin Wall Kimmerer

“I listen, I care, I understand, and I will work to find solutions suited only to you.”

— Carol Holland

The Journey to Botanicals

I consider myself a facilitator in the relationship between the plants and people. Since childhood, I have loved spending time in the woods, surrounded by majestic trees, mysterious plants, and fascinating wildlife. I currently live on a mountainside where, in addition to focusing on my clinical practice, I grow both native and medicinal plants that reveal their characters as I spend time amongst them, watching their growing habits, eating their berries, fruits or flowers, respectfully harvesting their different parts and making herbal restorative tonics and remedies.

Inspired by my Pharmacist father, I have always had a desire to use what I have been given to strive to improve the health of those around me.

Pacific Northwest — Inspiring the Practice

SERVICES

How I Offer Support

Personalized botanical support for your unique wellness goals

🌿

Immune Balance & Vitality

Signaling your body to find its natural balance when your immune system is struggling to mitigate disturbances. Personalized botanical regimens to assist your body in moderating immune response to reach a healthy balance of resilience, recovery, and vitality.

❤️

Cardio-Metabolic Energy

Enhancement of cardiac and metabolic function to improve energy stores and usage throughout your body. Botanicals through diet and supportive regimens combine with lifestyle tactics to help you gain energy and live your life with enthusiasm.

🧠

Mental Clarity & Digestive Harmony

Rebuilding your foundation when fatigue, stress, brain fog, or restless nights hold you back. Supporting healthy digestion complements recharging your brain for an integrated benefit. Custom formulas and strategies to help your body absorb and process nutrients more effectively, heal your gut, and regain a sense of calm.

How It Works

New to herbalism? Here's exactly what to expect — no surprises

1

Discovery Call

Free 15-20 min Telehealth chat. We talk about your concerns and discover how I will be able to help you.

2

Intake Forms

Complete Informed Consent, Client Intake, and HIPAA forms — so I have your full health picture.

3

Thorough Assessment

A thorough deep dive into your health story where we discuss all relevant factors, and I evaluate your health biometrics through any pertinent test results, analyze potential herb-drug-supplement interactions, and build your personalized botanical regimen.

4

Your Personal Plan

Custom botanical protocol, lifestyle recommendations, and a clear roadmap for your wellness journey.

5

Ongoing Support

Follow-up sessions to track progress, adjust formulas, and refine your plan as your body responds.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is herbalism right for me?
If you're dealing with chronic health challenges, feel stuck in a cycle of symptoms, or want a more natural approach alongside your current care — herbalism can help. A free Discovery Call is the easiest way to find out.
Do I need to stop my medications?
No. I always work alongside your existing care, never against it. Part of my intake process includes reviewing all your current medications and supplements to ensure safe, complementary support.
How long before I notice results?
Every person is different. Some clients notice shifts within 2–3 weeks; deeper patterns may take 2–3 months. We track progress at every follow-up.
Do you accept insurance?
I do not bill insurance directly, but I can provide documentation for HSA/FSA reimbursement where eligible.
Can we meet virtually?
Yes! All consultations are available via Zoom. I work with clients across Washington state and beyond.
BOTANICAL SANCTUARY

Botanical Sanctuary

Each plant in the sanctuary has a character that reveals itself over time — through growing habits, seasons, and the remedies it offers. These pages are a living guide to the botanicals I work with.

🌿 Baikal Skullcap

Anti-inflammatory powerhouse with a deep tradition in Chinese medicine. Supports immune modulation and respiratory health.

🌿 Comfrey

Known as "knitbone" for its remarkable ability to support tissue repair. Used topically for joint and muscle support.

🌿 Wild Rose

Rich in vitamin C and antioxidants. Traditional use for immune support, skin health, and gentle mood enhancement.

🌿 Solomon's Seal

Valued for musculoskeletal support — joints, tendons, and connective tissue. A woodland ally for structural resilience.
